back_translation: And this is the law of the guilt offering; it is most holy.
In the place where they kill the burnt offering, they shall kill the guilt offering, and its blood shall be sprinkled on all sides of the altar.
And all its fat shall be presented from it: the fat tail and the fat that covers the entrails.
And both kidneys with the fat that is on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which shall be removed with the kidneys.
And the priest shall burn these things as smoke on the altar, an offering by fire to the Lord. It is a guilt offering.
Every male among the priests shall eat it. It shall be eaten in a holy place; it is most holy.
The guilt offering is like the sin offering; one law applies to them both. The priest who makes atonement with it shall receive it as his own.
And the priest who offers the burnt offering of anyone, the hide of the burnt offering that he has offered shall be the priest's; it shall be his.
And every grain offering that is baked in an oven, and everything that is prepared in a deep pan or on a flat griddle, shall belong to the priest who offers it.
And every grain offering, whether mixed with oil or dry, shall belong to all the sons of Aaron, each one like his brother.
And this is the law of the peace offering that one shall present to the Lord.

If he offers it for thanksgiving, then together with the sacrifice of thanksgiving he shall present unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers coated with oil, and fine flour well-kneaded, cakes mixed with oil.
Along with cakes of leavened bread, he shall present his offering along with his peace offering of thanksgiving.
And he shall present from it one portion from every offering as a contribution to the Lord. It shall belong to the priest who sprinkles the blood of the peace offering.
And the meat of the sacrifice of his peace offering of thanksgiving shall be eaten on the day he offers it; he shall not leave any of it until morning.
And if the sacrifice he offers is a vow or a freewill offering, on the day he offers his sacrifice it shall be eaten, and on the next day what is left of it may also be eaten.
And what is left of the meat of the sacrifice on the third day shall be burned in fire.

And if any of the meat of his peace offering is indeed eaten on the third day, it shall not be accepted; the one who offered it shall not be credited with it; it shall be desecrated meat, and the person who eats from it shall bear his iniquity.
And the meat that touches anything unclean shall not be eaten; it shall be burned in fire. And the meat—everyone who is clean shall eat the meat.
And the person who eats meat from the peace offering that belongs to the Lord while his uncleanness is on him, that person shall be cut off from his people.

And the person who touches anything unclean—whether human uncleanness, an unclean animal, or any unclean abominable creature—and eats from the meat of the peace offering that belongs to the Lord, that person shall be cut off from his people.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: All the fat of an ox or sheep or goat, you shall not eat.
And the fat of a carcass and the fat of an animal torn by beasts may be used for any work, but you shall not eat it.
For everyone who eats the fat of an animal from which one may present an offering by fire to the Lord—the person who eats shall be cut off from his people.
And you shall not eat any blood in all your dwellings, of bird or of livestock.
Any person who eats any blood, that person shall be cut off from his people.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: The one who presents his peace offering to the Lord shall bring his offering to the Lord from his peace offering.
His own hands shall bring the offerings by fire of the Lord; the fat on the breast he shall bring, the breast, to wave it as a wave offering before the face of the Lord.
And the priest shall burn the fat as smoke on the altar, but the breast shall belong to Aaron and to his sons.
And the right thigh you shall give as a contribution to the priest from your peace offerings.
The one from the sons of Aaron who presents the blood of the peace offering and the fat shall have the right thigh as his portion.

For the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution I have taken from the sons of Israel, from their peace offerings, and I have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons as a permanent statute from the sons of Israel.
This is the anointing-portion of Aaron and the anointing-portion of his sons from the offerings by fire of the Lord, on the day He brought them near to serve as priests to the Lord.
Which the Lord commanded to be given to them on the day He anointed them, from the sons of Israel, a permanent statute for their generations.
This is the law for the burnt offering, for the grain offering, and for the sin offering, and for the guilt offering, and for the ordination offering, and for the peace offering.
Which the Lord commanded Moses on Mount Sinai, on the day He commanded the sons of Israel to present their offerings to the Lord in the wilderness of Sinai.
